texto: And another thing about German symphonic development: [it's] just like German philosophy, all worked out and systematized. When a German thinks, he reasons his way to a conclusion. Our Russian brother, on the other hand, starts with a conclusion and then might amuse himself with reasoning. Just keep one thing in mind. The creative act carries within itself its own aesthetic laws. When an artist revises, it means he is dissatisfied. When he revises what is already satisfying, he is Germanizing, chewing over what has [already] been said. We Russians are not cud chewers; we are omnivores!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Modest Mussorgsky: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “And another thing about German symphonic development: [it's] just like German philosophy, all wor…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, music, nationalism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e nationalism.

texto: Art is not an end in itself but a means of addressing humanity.
explicacao: Citação de Modest Mussorgsky sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “Art is not an end in itself but a means of addressing humanity.” articula autoestima, art, artist em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como termino e artist.
